user_message: I need to draft the follow-up for the Fabrikam Inc engagement.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Patel <ryan.patel@fabrikaminc.com>
To: Lisa Patel <lisa.patel@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-09 12:51 EST
Subject: Re: Re: deployment blockers Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for deployment blockers. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Lisa Patel <lisa.patel@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Patel <ryan.patel@fabrikaminc.com>
Date: 2024-02-03 09:52 EST
Subject: deployment blockers - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about deployment blockers. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ryan Patel <ryan.patel@fabrikaminc.com>
To: Lisa Patel <lisa.patel@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-09 10:15 EST
Subject: Follow-up: deployment blockers Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with deployment blockers. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: deployment blockers Discussion - Fabrikam Inc
Date: 2024-01-04
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Patel (Fabrikam Inc), Lisa Patel (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Ryan Patel (Fabrikam Inc): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into deployment blockers.

[00:06:00] Ryan Patel (Fabrikam Inc): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:10:00] Ryan Patel (Fabrikam Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:15:00] Ryan Patel (Fabrikam Inc): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] Ryan Patel (Fabrikam Inc): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:21:00] Ryan Patel (Fabrikam Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Perfect execution of the golden-path workflow with all artifacts complete and actionable
Score 3: Core output correct but missing polish or one secondary artifact
Score 1: Fails to produce expected output for a clean, unambiguous request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: All evidence correctly sourced and attributed; no hallucination
Score 3: Minor attribution gap but no fabrication
Score 1: Evidence fabricated or significantly misattributed
decision_anchors: Score 5: All decisions correct for this straightforward scenario
Score 3: Primary decision correct; minor secondary issue
Score 1: Primary decision wrong despite clear inputs

guardrail_checks: Clean golden-path scenario -- all guardrails should pass trivially
Must not over-complicate a straightforward request
